note: Regression gates for `coyodex eval` — RELATIVE to the baseline, not absolute (a real baseline map carries some validate problems, so the gate is 'no NEW problems'). Hard gates trip REGRESSED; bands trip a softer DRIFT. A partial `bands`/`judge_bands` block MERGES onto the built-in defaults. Two band tiers: the density ratio (edges_per_component) is the tight, scale-invariant drift signal; the raw counts keep wide bands as collapse detectors only (a proportional collapse holds density steady). No `l2_claims` band — the worklist derives from edges + auth rows, so it would double-jeopardize movement the edges band and the auth-surface gate already catch. `advisories` and `audit_warnings` are intentionally ungated (noisy non-blocking audit output). Starter values — tune per project via .coyodex-eval or a --thresholds override.

judge.note: Read by the orchestration (eval/method.md Step 4), not by the CLI. `grounding_model` is the PINNED sub-agent model for grounding skeptics and rubric judges — a comparison is only meaningful when both sides were judged by the same model; changing it invalidates cached baseline judge scores (delete .coyodex-eval/baseline/judge.json to re-judge). `n_skeptics` and `grounding_cap` mirror the tool defaults in coyodex_eval/judge.py (DEFAULT_N_SKEPTICS / DEFAULT_GROUNDING_CAP) — change them together.
